heading: Required counterintelligence assessments, briefings, notifications, and reports
label: 3371 Required counterintelligence assessments, briefings, notifications, and reports
notes: CodificationSection is comprised of . Subsec. (b) of enacted and amended the analysis for the National Security Act of 1947 ( et seq.).section 5304 of Pub. L. 116–92section 5304 of Pub. L. 116–92section 3111 of this title50 U.S.C. 3001
amendments: CodificationSection is comprised of . Subsec. (b) of enacted and amended the analysis for the National Security Act of 1947 ( et seq.).section 5304 of Pub. L. 116–92section 5304 of Pub. L. 116–92section 3111 of this title50 U.S.C. 3001

children.heading: Foreign counterintelligence and cybersecurity threats to Federal election campaigns
children.label: a Foreign counterintelligence and cybersecurity threats to Federal election campaigns
i
children.text: A description of foreign counterintelligence and cybersecurity threats to campaigns of candidates for Federal office.

children.label: ii
children.text: A summary of best practices that campaigns of candidates for Federal office can employ in seeking to counter such threats.

children.label: iii
children.text: An identification of publicly available resources, including United States Government resources, for countering such threats.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided by clause (ii), with respect to an election for Federal office, a report under this subsection shall be first made available not later than the date that is 1 year before the date of such election, and may be subsequently revised as the Director of National Intelligence determines appropriate.

children.heading: 2020 elections
children.label: ii 2020 elections
children.text: With respect to an election for Federal office that occurs during 2020, the report under this subsection shall be first made available not later than the date that is 60 days after , and may be subsequently revised as the Director of National Intelligence determines appropriate.December 20, 2020

children.heading: Information to be included
children.label: C Information to be included
children.text: A report under this subsection shall reflect the most current information available to the Director of National Intelligence regarding foreign counterintelligence and cybersecurity threats.

children.heading: Treatment of campaigns subject to heightened threats
children.label: 2 Treatment of campaigns subject to heightened threats
children.text: If the Director of the Federal Bureau of Investigation and the Under Secretary of Homeland Security for Intelligence and Analysis jointly determine that a campaign of a candidate for Federal office is subject to a heightened foreign counterintelligence or cybersecurity threat, the Director and the Under Secretary, consistent with the protection of sources and methods, may make available additional information to the appropriate representatives of such campaign.

children.heading: Director of National Intelligence assessment of foreign interference in Federal elections
children.label: c Director of National Intelligence assessment of foreign interference in Federal elections
A
children.text: conduct an assessment of any information indicating that a foreign government, or any person acting as an agent of or on behalf of a foreign government, has acted with the intent or purpose of interfering in elections for Federal office occurring during the Federal election cycle; and

children.label: i
children.text: The President.

children.label: ii
children.text: The Secretary of State.

children.label: iii
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury.

children.label: iv
children.text: The Secretary of Defense.

children.label: v
children.text: The Attorney General.

children.label: vi
children.text: The Secretary of Homeland Security.

children.label: vii
children.text: Congress.

children.label: A
children.text: The nature of any foreign interference and any methods employed to execute the act.

children.label: B
children.text: The persons involved.

children.label: C
children.text: The foreign government or governments that authorized, directed, sponsored, or supported the act.

children.heading: Publication
children.label: 3 Publication
children.text: The Director shall, not later than 60 days after the end of a Federal election cycle, make available to the public, to the greatest extent possible consistent with the protection of sources and methods, the findings transmitted under paragraph (1)(B).

children.heading: Federal election cycle defined
children.label: 4 Federal election cycle defined
children.text: In this section, the term “Federal election cycle” means the period which begins on the day after the date of a regularly scheduled general election for Federal office and which ends on the date of the first regularly scheduled general election for Federal office held after such date.

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: 5 Effective date
children.text: This subsection shall apply with respect to the Federal election cycle that began during November 2018, and each succeeding Federal election cycle.
